perturbation.original_text: 1.1 iBio hereby grants to CC-Pharming for the term of this Agreement, a nonexclusive, non- assignable, non-sublicensable, limited right and license to use iBio's Technology in order to manufacture, process, prepare, and obtain regulatory approval for the development and production of Product(s) and work to be performed under this Agreement. Each Product will be set forth in a separate Statement of Work in the form set forth in Appendix A, which will be signed by representatives of each Party with signing authority. Each phase of the project(s) will be subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement unless otherwise provided for or in a Statement of Work ("SOW"), including but not limited to project purpose, project phases, and a project budget.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 iBio hereby grants to CC-Pharming for the term of this Agreement, a nonexclusive, non- assignable, non-sublicensable, limited right and license to use iBio's Technology in order to manufacture, process, prepare, and obtain regulatory approval for the development and production of Product(s) and work to be performed under this Agreement. Each phase of the project(s) will be subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement unless otherwise provided for or in a Statement of Work ("SOW"), including but not limited to project purpose, project phases, and a project budget.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Each Product will be set forth in a separate Statement of Work in the form set forth in Appendix A, which will be signed by representatives of each Party with signing authority.', the contract creates an ambiguity regarding how specific projects are defined and authorized. This omission contradicts later sections that refer to the SOW as the defining document for project specifics. For example, Section 2.1 refers to 'each Statement of Work', which is now undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Project Technical Coordinators will be appointed by each the Parties for each Statement of Work. The Project Technical Coordinators will be responsible for exchanging information with the other Party, coordinating any visits and arranging all other matters pertinent to that Statement of Work.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that 'Each Product will be set forth in a separate Statement of Work in the form set forth in Appendix A, which will be signed by representatives of each Party with signing authority.' This clause provided a clear mechanism for defining and authorizing specific projects. The changed text removes this clause, creating ambiguity about how specific projects are now defined and authorized. This directly contradicts Section 2.1, which refers to 'each Statement of Work'. Since the SOW is no longer defined, the meaning of Section 2.1 becomes unclear and unenforceable. This omission creates a strong in-text contradiction, impacting the clarity and enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Upon execution of this Agreement and acceptance of iBio's proposal for the development of a Plant-Made Rituximab, and in consideration for providing the technology transfer contemplated herein, CC-Pharming shall pay iBio [***], which shall be paid as follows: 3.1.1 First payment of [***] is required to initiate the project: [***]. 3.1.2 Second payment of [***]. Due upon presentation of the following deliverables: a) Detailed design drawings of the pilot plant; b) Schematic design of the commercial facility; c) Purified antibody for pre-clinical testing and development; and d) The quality management system (QMS) development up to and including governance documents and governance standard operating procedures (SOP's). 3.1.3 Final payment of [***], due upon presentation of the following deliverables: a) Detailed Design drawings for the commercial facility; b) Completion of QMS documentation for rituximab including batch records, release documents, and assay SOPs; c) Completion of all training sessions and training documentation; d) Delivery of antibody drug substance for clinical trials; and e) Completion of a chemistry, manufacturing, and controls (CMC) document to support an (investigational new drug application (IND) or equivalent.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Upon execution of this Agreement and acceptance of iBio's proposal for the development of a Plant-Made Rituximab, and in consideration for providing the technology transfer contemplated herein, CC-Pharming shall pay iBio [***].
perturbation.explanation: By removing the payment schedule and deliverables detailed in sections 3.1.1, 3.1.2, and 3.1.3, the contract now lacks specific conditions for payment. This creates an in-text contradiction because section 8.2 now referencing to prototypes, are not clear when the payment for them are due, making it difficult to enforce what actions constitude a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 EACH PARTY ALSO SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIMS ANY GUARANTEE THAT IT WILL BE ABLE TO SUCCESSFULLY ACHIEVE THE DESIRED RESULTS REGARDING THE WORK UNDER ANY STATEMENT OF WORK, OR THAT ANY PROTOTYPE(S) WHICH MAY BE DEVELOPED PURSUANT TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL MEET ANY DEVELOPMENT OBJECTIVES, OR ANY REQUIREMENTS OF EITHER PARTY. THE FOREGOING NOTWITHSTANDING, EACH PARTY WILL MAKE REASONABLE GOOD FAITH EFFORTS TO COMPLETE THE ACTIVITIES DESCRIBED IN THE STATEMENTS OF WORK. FAILURE TO ACHIEVE THE DESIRED RESULTS UNDER A STATEMENT OF WORK DOES NOT CONSTITUTE BREACH OF CONTRACT.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the payment schedule and deliverables in section 3.1 introduces a strong in-text contradiction with section 8.2. Specifically, section 8.2 mentions 'PROTOTYPE(S) WHICH MAY BE DEVELOPED PURSUANT TO THIS AGREEMENT'. With the original payment schedule in place, it was clear when payments relating to prototypes (deliverables) were due. Now, with the payment schedule removed in the changed text, it's unclear when payments for prototypes are due, making it difficult to determine if failure to pay constitutes a breach, thus contradicting section 8.2's assertion that 'FAILURE TO ACHIEVE THE DESIRED RESULTS UNDER A STATEMENT OF WORK DOES NOT CONSTITUTE BREACH OF CONTRACT.' This represents a clear and direct conflict creating significant uncertainty and potential unenforceability, because the triggers for payment are removed.

perturbation.original_text: 7.6 Confidential Information must be identified as Confidential at the time of disclosure, and all material containing Confidential Information must have a restrictive marking. Any Confidential Information disclosed orally or visually must be summarized by the disclosing Party in writing and the writing must be provided to the receiving Party within twenty (20) days after the disclosure. In the case of inadvertent disclosure of Confidential information that was not marked as Confidential, the Disclosing Party has ten (10) business days from the time they discover that the information should have been marked Confidential, to inform the other Party of such a designation, and the parties agree to retroactively mark any such information as Confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.6 Confidential Information must be identified as Confidential at the time of disclosure, and all material containing Confidential Information must have a restrictive marking.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the guidelines for oral or visual disclosures not written, it creates a loophole that enables confidential information to be transferred orally, without the disclosing party having to worry about it being protected under the confidentialty agreement, if not marked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3 Each of the parties agrees that for five (5) years from the date of disclosure, the receiving Party agrees to limit disclosure of the disclosing Party's Confidential Information to those of the receiving party's employees and contractors, and employees and contractors of its Subsidiaries, who have a need to know it, and the receiving Party agrees to use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination outside of those employees and contractors as the receiving Party does with similar information of its own which it does not desire to publish, disclose or disseminate.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies how orally or visually disclosed confidential information should be handled (summarized in writing and provided within 20 days). The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text (7.3) states that the receiving party must limit disclosure of 'the disclosing Party's Confidential Information'. By removing the requirement to summarize oral/visual disclosures in writing, the changed text creates a loophole where orally disclosed information, if not marked, may not be treated as 'Confidential Information' under section 7.3, creating a contradiction because the receiving party is no longer obligated to protect it, and making the obligations in 7.3 unclear or unenforceable with respect to oral disclosures.
